stakeholders.name: Interim Iranian Leadership Council (Pezeshkian, Mohseni-Ejei, Arafi)
stakeholders.position: Attempting to maintain regime continuity while managing succession crisis

stakeholders.name: Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC)
stakeholders.position: Hardline resistance to foreign intervention, positioned to seize power

stakeholders.name: Ali Larijani
stakeholders.role: Senior political figure with military influence
stakeholders.position: Unclear but reportedly controlling military operations

stakeholders.name: Assembly of Experts
stakeholders.role: Constitutional body for selecting Supreme Leader
stakeholders.position: Disrupted by Israeli strikes, uncertain operational capacity

stakeholders.name: Trump Administration
stakeholders.position: Escalating objectives from nuclear threats to regime change denial to freedom promotion

stakeholders.name: Israeli Government
stakeholders.position: Supporting U.S. operations, targeting Iranian leadership infrastructure

stakeholders.name: Iranian Democratic Opposition
stakeholders.position: Fragmented and lacking unified leadership or credible organization

stakeholders.name: Regional Gulf States
stakeholders.position: Vulnerable to escalation, likely seeking de-escalation

predictions.text: IRGC hardliners will attempt to consolidate power, potentially sidelining the interim civilian council
predictions.basis: Sources identify IRGC as 'in the best position to take power' and they remain 'well armed and full of hard-liners'

predictions.text: Iran will escalate ballistic missile attacks on Israeli and U.S. targets in response to continued strikes
predictions.basis: Iran has already threatened 'ferocious' retaliation and is currently firing missiles at targets

predictions.text: Trump will authorize the threatened 'third wave' of strikes targeting remaining leadership candidates
predictions.basis: Trump explicitly warned potential successors and stated objectives have been escalating

predictions.text: Iran will experience significant internal instability or potential state collapse
predictions.basis: Leadership decimation, lack of unified opposition, and analysts warning of failed state scenario

predictions.text: U.S. will deploy ground troops despite public opposition
predictions.basis: Pentagon has not ruled it out but operation is 'broadly unpopular among Americans'

historicalContext: This represents the most significant disruption to Iran's theocratic system since the 1979 Islamic Revolution. Khamenei's 40-year rule provided unprecedented continuity to the regime, and his elimination creates a succession crisis without historical precedent in the Islamic Republic. The targeting of the Assembly of Experts echoes but exceeds the disruption of Iraq's Baath Party leadership structure in 2003. The escalation follows a pattern beginning with December protests that the regime violently suppressed, crossing Trump's stated red line. Previous U.S. operations like 'Midnight Hammer' had targeted Iran's nuclear capabilities, but this represents the first direct targeting of Iran's supreme leadership structure since the revolution.
implications: Regional destabilization as Iran's proxy network loses central coordination and control
Potential nuclear proliferation crisis if Iran's weapons program proceeds without centralized oversight
Refugee crisis if Iranian state institutions collapse completely
Escalation of U.S.-Iran conflict potentially drawing in regional allies and adversaries
Precedent for targeted elimination of foreign government leadership structures
Disruption of global energy markets due to attacks on Gulf state infrastructure
Strengthening of hardline military elements over civilian governance in Iran
Potential power vacuum enabling terrorist groups or regional competitors to expand influence
